diagrams.title: ACID Properties Overview
diagrams.description: A comprehensive diagram showing all four ACID properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) as pillars supporting the transaction concept, with visual icons for each property to help students recognize and remember each component.
elements: Icon for Atomicity (all-or-nothing symbol like a coin)
Connecting lines showing all properties belong to a transaction
diagrams.purpose: Provides a high-level visual map of all four ACID properties, establishing the foundational概念 for understanding transaction management in databases

diagrams.title: Atomicity - All or Nothing
diagrams.description: A flowchart or decision diagram illustrating how a transaction with multiple operations (A, B, C) either completes all operations successfully or rolls back all changes, demonstrating the all-or-nothing nature of atomicity.
elements: Operation steps (Step A, Step B, Step C)
Success path showing all operations completing
Visual comparison showing partial completion is impossible
diagrams.purpose: Explains the atomicity property by showing that transactions are indivisible units - either all operations execute or none do, preventing partial updates that could corrupt data

diagrams.title: Isolation with Concurrency Control
diagrams.description: A timeline or interleaving diagram showing multiple concurrent transactions (T1, T2) and how isolation ensures each transaction appears to execute alone, with visual representation of serializability and potential race conditions.
elements: Two or more parallel transaction timelines (T1, T2)
Final committed state showing all transactions completed correctly
Optional: showing dirty read, non-repeatable read scenarios
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how isolation prevents concurrent transactions from interfering with each other, ensuring transactions appear to execute in a serial order even when run concurrently
